preamble: Part of Grimm's Law: PIE voiced plosives became voiceless plosives in Germanic.

examples.note: Final consonant devoicing: word-final /b/ → [p] in Turkish (kitap 'book'). In suffixed forms: kitab-ı (ACC), showing underlying /b/.
PIE plain voiced *b → PGmc voiceless *p. Cf. Lithuanian dubùs 'hollow, deep' preserving original *b.
PIE *b → PGmc *p; PGmc *slēpana- → OE slǣpan → sleep.

sources: Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction. MIT Press.
